data.title: Sell Rating on Fresnillo: Modest Cash Flow Yield and Operational Disruptions Drive Preference for Higher-Yield Peers
data.description:
Ioannis Masvoulas, an analyst from Morgan Stanley, maintained the Sell rating on FRESNILLO. The associated price target is p2,400.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ioannis Masvoulas has given his Sell rating due to a combination of factors tied to Fresnillo’s recent operational and financial profile. First-quarter 2026 silver-equivalent output slightly lagged market expectations, with silver volumes missing consensus because of weaker grades and disruptions at key mines such as Saucito and Fresnillo, while the positive contributions from gold and by-products left the near-term earnings effect largely negligible.

He also highlights that, at prevailing commodity prices, the shares only offer an estimated 2026 free cash flow yield of about 6%, which he views as unappealing versus alternatives. In particular, he prefers Endeavour Mining, which he estimates to generate roughly double the free cash flow yield, reinforcing a relative Underweight stance on Fresnillo despite stable medium-term guidance and largely temporary operational issues.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: RELX: Strengthening Legal AI Advantage Drives Buy Rating at Attractive Valuation
data.description:
Bank of America Securities analyst David Amiras has maintained their bullish stance on RLXXF stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Amiras has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily the strengthening competitive position of RELX’s Legal division as generic AI tools show their limits in high-stakes legal work. High-profile incidents of AI “hallucinations” in U.S. court filings underscore the need for trusted, domain-specific solutions that are built on verifiable, high-quality legal content, an area where RELX’s Lexis+ with Protégé is particularly well positioned.

Amiras also highlights that, despite a solid outlook including an expected resilient 5-year EPS compound annual growth rate of around 12%, RELX’s shares are valued at about 19 times projected 2026 earnings, a level last seen in 2020. Given this combination of robust growth prospects, rising demand for specialized legal AI tools, and what he regards as a still-attractive valuation, he reiterates a Buy rating and maintains a 3,800 GBp price objective.

In another report released yesterday, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a £30.95 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Meta Platforms: Strong Ad Momentum and Aggressive AI Investments Underpin Buy Rating
data.description:
Analyst Brian White of Monness maintained a Buy rating on Meta Platforms, retaining the price target of $890.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Brian White has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Meta’s strong growth profile and aggressive AI strategy. He expects first‑quarter revenue to rise roughly 31% year over year, outpacing recent quarters and reflecting solid momentum in the core advertising business, while management continues to drive efficiency and expand the platform across key properties.

At the same time, White points to Meta’s rapid build‑out of artificial intelligence capabilities, including new in‑house models, custom MTIA chips, and collaborations with major semiconductor partners such as Broadcom, Arm, AMD, and NVIDIA. Although regulatory and macroeconomic risks remain, he believes Meta’s substantial AI investments and continued ad strength position the company for robust revenue and earnings growth through 2026, justifying a Buy rating on the stock.

In another report released yesterday, UBS also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $908.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
